name: Andhra Pradesh (ఆంధ్ర ప్రదేశ్)
summary: Andhra Pradesh (ఆంధ్ర ప్రదేశ్ in Telugu) is a South Indian common-law constituent state of the Indian Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-D special-status framework explicitly providing for the equitable allocation of opportunities in employment and education across the different regions of the state through state-mandated regional-allocation rules, as the only Indian state to have undergone a major formal bifurcation in modern Indian state-formation history (Telangana separation effective 2 June 2014 under the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2014 — creating Telangana as the 29th Indian state), and as the central jurisdiction of Indian Andhra Pradesh High Court Six-Point Formula 1973 jurisprudence regarding regional-equality framework (which became Article 371-D in 1973). The Article 371-D framework establishes mandatory regional-allocation rules for employment in state services and admissions to state-funded educational institutions across the four traditional regions of pre-2014 Andhra Pradesh (Coastal Andhra, Rayalaseema, Telangana, and the Hyderabad-Karnataka region) — preserved post-2014 bifurcation. Andhra Pradesh comprises ~162,968 km² with a population of ~50 million, making it the seventh-largest Indian state by area and tenth-largest by population. Family-law framework operates under the federal Indian common-law framework (Hindu Marriage Act 1955, Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937, Guardians and Wards Act 1890) applied via state administrative framework. The Andhra Pradesh High Court (established 1 January 2019 at Amaravati, following bifurcation with Telangana) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Andhra Pradesh is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Andhra Pradesh is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of India Article 371-D (Andhra Pradesh Special Provisions)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of India Article 371-D
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Article inserted by Constitution (Thirty-Second Amendment) Act 1973 establishing special provisions for Andhra Pradesh — equitable allocation of opportunities in employment and education across different regions of the state.

statutory_framework.title: Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act of 2 June 2014 establishing the State of Telangana via bifurcation from Andhra Pradesh — only major modern formal bifurcation in Indian state-formation history.

statutory_framework.title: Six-Point Formula
statutory_framework.relevance: Foundational Indian Government framework establishing principles for Article 371-D regional-equality framework — basis for Article 371-D constitutional provision.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution 32nd Amendment Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Amendment Act of 1973 inserting Article 371-D providing regional-equality framework for Andhra Pradesh.

apex_courts.name: Andhra Pradesh High Court
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Andhra Pradesh — established 1 January 2019 following Telangana bifurcation.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Andhra Pradesh.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Andhra Pradesh.

anonymisation_convention: Andhra Pradesh family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Hyderabad-State-+-Operation-Polo + integration-of-Princely-State framework
key_developments.description: Hyderabad State Operation Polo 17 September 1948 ending Nizam-rule + integration of Princely State of Hyderabad into India — substantively distinctive South Indian post-independence-integration framework. Substantive demographic and constitutional-administrative-trajectory framework affecting subsequent 1956 States Reorganisation framework.

key_developments.title: Andhra-State-+-language-based-state-creation framework
key_developments.description: Andhra State established 1 October 1953 separating from Madras State following Potti Sriramulu hunger strike death — substantively distinctive globally first-language-based-state-creation-in-modern-India framework. Foundational pre-1956-Andhra-Pradesh-merger framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Andhra Pradesh State Reorganisation + States-Reorganisation-Act framework
key_developments.description: Andhra Pradesh formed via States Reorganisation Act 1956 effective 1 November 1956 — substantively distinctive South Indian merging-Telugu-speaking-regions-including-Telangana-from-Hyderabad-State framework. Foundational substantive Telugu-speaking-state framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and 1969 Jai Telangana movement.

key_developments.title: Telangana movement + Jai-Telangana-separate-statehood-movement framework
key_developments.description: Jai Telangana movement 1969 — substantively distinctive globally beginning-of-45-year-Telangana-separate-statehood-movement framework. Foundational pre-2014-bifurcation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Six-Point Formula + Constitution 32nd Amendment + Article 371-D framework
key_developments.description: Six-Point Formula 1973 + Constitution (Thirty-Second Amendment) Act 1973 inserting Article 371-D providing regional-equality framework — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-Indian-Constitutional-framework-based-on-formal-Six-Point-Formula-regional-equality framework. Foundational substantive Article 371-D framework persisting through 2014 bifurcation.

key_developments.title: India ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Andhra-Pradesh-applicability framework
key_developments.description: India ratified UNCRC 11 December 1992 with applicability to Andhra Pradesh — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Indian-derivative-common-law framework applicable to Andhra Pradesh. Foundational substantive child-rights framework integration affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: K-Chandrashekar-Rao-fast-+-Telangana-Andolanam-+-Srikrishna-Committee framework
key_developments.description: K Chandrashekar Rao fast 29 November 2009 substantively reinvigorating Telangana Andolanam — substantively distinctive South Indian Telangana-separate-statehood-movement reinvigoration framework. Subsequent Srikrishna Committee 2010 + 2013 announcement of bifurcation framework. Foundational pre-2014-Reorganisation-Act framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act + Telangana-bifurcation-+-29th-Indian-state framework
key_developments.description: Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2014 effective 2 June 2014 — substantively distinctive globally only-Indian-state-to-have-undergone-major-formal-bifurcation-in-modern-Indian-state-formation-history framework. Creating Telangana as the 29th Indian state via bifurcation from Andhra Pradesh. Foundational substantive post-bifurcation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Andhra Pradesh High Court established at Amaravati + dual-state-High-Court-establishment framework
key_developments.description: Andhra Pradesh High Court established 1 January 2019 at Amaravati following bifurcation with Telangana High Court at Hyderabad — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-major-formal-bifurcation-requiring-dual-state-High-Court-establishment framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Reddy government framework + Amaravati capital framework + AP-Telangana-dispute resolution
key_developments.description: Y. S. Jagan Mohan Reddy (YSRCP) Chief Ministership 2019-2024 framework consolidation through 2023. Amaravati capital framework substantively significant institutional context. AP-Telangana-dispute resolution framework substantively significant institutional context affecting cross-state family-law adjudication. Substantively significant South Indian Indian state institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Andhra Pradesh High Court + Supreme Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + continuing-Article-371-D-+-post-bifurcation-Andhra-Pradesh-state-framework framework
key_developments.description: Andhra Pradesh High Court (Amaravati) and Supreme Court of India continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Guardians and Wards Act 1890 + Article 371-D framework in custody disputes within continuing post-bifurcation Andhra Pradesh state framework + Article 371-D regional-equality framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Andhra Pradesh operates a federal Indian common-law framework — places Andhra Pradesh in the South Indian state cluster.

Only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-D special-status framework explicitly providing for equitable allocation of opportunities in employment and education across different regions is structurally distinctive globally — only modern multi-state framework with intra-state regional-equality constitutional structure.
Only Indian state to have undergone major formal bifurcation in modern Indian state-formation history (Telangana 2014) is structurally distinctive globally.
Six-Point Formula 1973 jurisprudence is structurally distinctive within Indian Constitutional framework — only modern Constitutional framework based on formal regional-equality formula.
Article 371-D pre-2014 bifurcation preservation in post-bifurcation Andhra Pradesh state framework is structurally distinctive.

Andhra Pradesh High Court establishment at Amaravati 2019 + Telangana High Court establishment at Hyderabad is structurally distinctive within Indian state High Court establishment cluster — only modern major formal bifurcation requiring dual-state High Court establishment.
editorial_notes: Andhra Pradesh jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Hyderabad-State-Operation-Polo-1948-to-continuing-post-bifurcation trajectory: 1948-Hyderabad-State-+-Operation-Polo + 1953-Andhra-State-+-language-based-state-creation + 1956-Andhra-Pradesh-State-Reorganisation + 1969-Telangana-movement-Jai-Telangana + 1973-Six-Point-Formula-+-Article-371-D + 1992-India-UNCRC-ratification + 2009-K-Chandrashekar-Rao-fast-+-Telangana-Andolanam + 2014-Andhra-Pradesh-Reorganisation-Act-+-Telangana-bifurcation + 2019-Andhra-Pradesh-High-Court-Amaravati + 2024-Andhra-Pradesh-High-Court-+-Supreme-Court-+-Article-371-D-framework.

Common-law South Indian Indian-state (Indian Constitution Article 371-D + Constitution 32nd Amendment Act 1973 + Six-Point Formula 1973 + Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2014 Telangana bifurcation + Andhra Pradesh High Court 2019 establishment at Amaravati + Indian non-Hague-1980). Only Indian state operating Article 371-D regional-equality constitutional framework globally + only Indian state to have undergone major formal bifurcation in modern Indian state-formation history (Telangana 2014) + only modern Indian Constitutional framework based on formal Six-Point Formula regional-equality framework + only modern major formal bifurcation requiring dual-state High Court establishment. Completes major Indian Article 371 special-status framework cluster (Sikkim 371-F + Nagaland 371-A + Mizoram 371-G + Arunachal Pradesh 371-H + Manipur 371-C + Andhra Pradesh 371-D).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Hindu Marriage Act 1955 + Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 + Guardians and Wards Act 1890 + Article 371-D framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins South-Indian + common-law + Indian-state-cluster + Article-371-D-regional-equality-framework-globally-distinctive + Six-Point-Formula-1973-globally-distinctive + 2014-Telangana-bifurcation-only-modern-formal-bifurcation-globally-distinctive + Hyderabad-State-Operation-Polo-1948 + Andhra-State-1953-+-Potti-Sriramulu-first-language-based-state-creation + States-Reorganisation-Act-1956 + Jai-Telangana-movement-1969 + Article-371-D-1973-+-Constitution-32nd-Amendment + K-Chandrashekar-Rao-fast-2009-+-Telangana-Andolanam + Srikrishna-Committee-2010 + Andhra-Pradesh-Reorganisation-Act-2014 + Andhra-Pradesh-High-Court-2019-Amaravati-+-Telangana-High-Court-Hyderabad-dual-state-High-Court-establishment-globally-distinctive + Indian-Article-371-cluster-completion-Sikkim-Nagaland-Mizoram-Arunachal-Pradesh-Manipur-Andhra-Pradesh + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
